patch.value: cli/main.ts commander program is a module-level singleton: dynamically registered extension commands/flags persist across in-process invocations

Third member of the pm-8fxc re-entrancy class, found while validating the pm-kvd0 worker bridge: 'const program = new Command()' lives at module scope (src/cli/main.ts:2494), and registerDynamicExtensionCommandPaths/addRuntimeFieldOption mutate that singleton per invocation. One-shot processes discard the mutations by exiting, but any long-lived host observes cross-invocation leakage: a kit extension registering --kit-note on core 'list' in workspace A makes 'pm list --type Task' in unrelated workspace B fail usage-validation ('Unknown option --type for extension command list. Expected one of: --kit-note'), and a 'pm --no-extensions beads import' after an earlier beads install in another workspace returns generic-failure instead of unknown_command. The pm-8fxc entry resets (snapshot caches + active-extension registries) cannot cover commander's own command/option registry. Interim isolation: the test bridge disposes its worker per test context. Real fix: construct the program per invocation inside runPmCli (or an unregister seam for dynamic paths) — a prerequisite for true SDK embedding of the CLI runtime; sequence with the pm-yh8r decomposition seam and pm-usfg SDK-first slices.

runPmCli invocations are fully re-entrant in one process: dynamic extension commands/flags registered by invocation N are invisible to invocation N+1 unless re-discovered from the active workspace; existing one-shot CLI behavior and startup latency unchanged; test bridge no longer needs per-test worker disposal

value.note: module-level 'const program = new Command()' (line ~2494) + registerDynamicExtensionCommandPaths mutations
